libraryPreparationPreflight.agent_guidance: When exercising Lucene core paths such as MMapDirectory unmapping or RamUsageEstimator on modern JDKs, keep/add test JVM args: --add-opens=java.base/java.nio=ALL-UNNAMED and --add-opens=java.base/jdk.internal.ref=ALL-UNNAMED. For the javac failure itself, adapt test custom AttributeImpl subclasses for Lucene 6.0.0 by implementing reflectWith(AttributeReflector); the pre-6 default reflection behavior is no longer available.

input_evidence.name: issue
input_evidence.summary: #5851 [Automation] javac compile fails for org.apache.lucene:lucene-core:6.0.0; label=fails-javac-compile; body_chars=8015

risks: Adding lucene-analyzers-common or other Lucene modules is not required for core-only reachability coverage and may unnecessarily broaden the workflow scope.
If the JVM opens are dropped, tests covering MMapDirectory/RamUsageEstimator internals may fail or avoid the reachability paths already covered by the existing scaffold.
The reported compile failure is an API change in AttributeImpl, not evidence of a missing dependency or Docker service.
libraryPreparationPreflight.summary: lucene-core 6.0.0 is a standalone artifact with no Maven dependencies or external service needs, but meaningful existing coverage touches JDK-internal-access paths that require JVM module opens.
